title: Đăng ký user
requirement: Đăng ký user với google, github, facebook

userStories.title: Register with Google
userStories.story: As an end-user, I want to register using my Google account, so that I can quickly create an account without entering my details manually.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Given the user is on the registration page, When the user clicks 'Register with Google' and successfully authenticates with Google, Then the system should create a new user account using the email and name from Google, or merge with an existing account if the email already exists.
Given the user is already registered with the same email via another provider, When the user registers with Google, Then the system should merge the accounts and allow login with either provider.
Given the user cancels the Google authentication process, When the user returns to the registration page, Then no account should be created and the user should remain unauthenticated.

testCases.title: Successful Google Registration
testCases.steps: 1. Navigate to the registration page. 2. Click 'Register with Google'. 3. Authenticate with a valid Google account. 4. Grant necessary permissions.
testCases.expectedResult: User account is created with email and name from Google. User is logged in.

testCases.title: Google Registration with Existing Email
testCases.steps: 1. Register an account with email 'user@example.com' via GitHub. 2. Log out. 3. Click 'Register with Google'. 4. Authenticate with Google account using 'user@example.com'.
testCases.expectedResult: Accounts are merged. User can log in with either Google or GitHub. No duplicate accounts are created.

testCases.title: Google Registration Cancelled
testCases.steps: 1. Navigate to the registration page. 2. Click 'Register with Google'. 3. Cancel the authentication process.
testCases.expectedResult: No account is created. User remains on the registration page.

userStories.title: Register with GitHub
userStories.story: As an end-user, I want to register using my GitHub account, so that I can quickly create an account without entering my details manually.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Given the user is on the registration page, When the user clicks 'Register with GitHub' and successfully authenticates with GitHub, Then the system should create a new user account using the email and name from GitHub, or merge with an existing account if the email already exists.
Given the user is already registered with the same email via another provider, When the user registers with GitHub, Then the system should merge the accounts and allow login with either provider.
Given the user cancels the GitHub authentication process, When the user returns to the registration page, Then no account should be created and the user should remain unauthenticated.

testCases.title: Successful GitHub Registration
testCases.steps: 1. Navigate to the registration page. 2. Click 'Register with GitHub'. 3. Authenticate with a valid GitHub account. 4. Grant necessary permissions.
testCases.expectedResult: User account is created with email and name from GitHub. User is logged in.

testCases.title: GitHub Registration with Existing Email
testCases.steps: 1. Register an account with email 'user@example.com' via Facebook. 2. Log out. 3. Click 'Register with GitHub'. 4. Authenticate with GitHub account using 'user@example.com'.
testCases.expectedResult: Accounts are merged. User can log in with either Facebook or GitHub. No duplicate accounts are created.

testCases.title: GitHub Registration Cancelled
testCases.steps: 1. Navigate to the registration page. 2. Click 'Register with GitHub'. 3. Cancel the authentication process.
testCases.expectedResult: No account is created. User remains on the registration page.

userStories.title: Register with Facebook
userStories.story: As an end-user, I want to register using my Facebook account, so that I can quickly create an account without entering my details manually.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Given the user is on the registration page, When the user clicks 'Register with Facebook' and successfully authenticates with Facebook, Then the system should create a new user account using the email and name from Facebook, or merge with an existing account if the email already exists.
Given the user is already registered with the same email via another provider, When the user registers with Facebook, Then the system should merge the accounts and allow login with either provider.
Given the user cancels the Facebook authentication process, When the user returns to the registration page, Then no account should be created and the user should remain unauthenticated.

testCases.title: Successful Facebook Registration
testCases.steps: 1. Navigate to the registration page. 2. Click 'Register with Facebook'. 3. Authenticate with a valid Facebook account. 4. Grant necessary permissions.
testCases.expectedResult: User account is created with email and name from Facebook. User is logged in.

testCases.title: Facebook Registration with Existing Email
testCases.steps: 1. Register an account with email 'user@example.com' via Google. 2. Log out. 3. Click 'Register with Facebook'. 4. Authenticate with Facebook account using 'user@example.com'.
testCases.expectedResult: Accounts are merged. User can log in with either Google or Facebook. No duplicate accounts are created.

testCases.steps: 1. Navigate to the registration page. 2. Click 'Register with Facebook'. 3. Cancel the authentication process.
testCases.expectedResult: No account is created. User remains on the registration page.

userStories.title: Prevent Duplicate Accounts Across Providers
userStories.story: As an end-user, I want the system to merge my accounts if I register with the same email via different providers, so that I have a single account regardless of the provider used.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Given a user has already registered with one provider, When the user registers with another provider using the same email, Then the system should merge the accounts and allow login with any linked provider.

testCases.title: Merge Accounts on Same Email
testCases.steps: 1. Register with Google using 'user@example.com'. 2. Log out. 3. Register with GitHub using 'user@example.com'.
testCases.expectedResult: Only one user account exists. User can log in with either Google or GitHub.

userStories.title: Store User Information from Social Providers
userStories.story: As an end-user, I want my email and name to be stored when I register via a social provider, so that my account information is accurate and consistent.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Given the user registers via Google, GitHub, or Facebook, When the registration is successful, Then the system should store the user's email and name as provided by the social provider.

testCases.title: Store Email and Name
testCases.steps: 1. Register with any supported social provider. 2. Check the user profile data.
testCases.expectedResult: User's email and name match the data from the social provider.
